items.q: Why was Jenner's smallpox vaccination significant in 1796?
items.correct: He showed cowpox material could prevent smallpox safely — the first effective vaccine, though he did not know why it worked; germ theory came nearly a century later.
items.misconception: Jenner discovered germs and used them to make vaccines.
items.random_wrong: Jenner used penicillin to cure smallpox.
items.why: Jenner worked empirically, not from germ theory which was Pasteur's. Students collapse the discoveries together; vaccination predates the science explaining it.

items.q: Did the Wall Street Crash cause the Great Depression?
items.correct: It triggered it but the underlying causes were over-production, agricultural slump, weak banking regulation, and uneven wealth — the Crash exposed structural problems.
items.misconception: The Wall Street Crash on its own caused the Great Depression.
items.random_wrong: The Wall Street Crash happened because the US joined WWII.
items.why: The 1920s boom hid weaknesses (unequal wealth, fragile banks). The Crash was a symptom and accelerant, not a sole cause — important for understanding the New Deal.

items.q: Why did Chartism fail in the short term?
items.correct: Mass petitions were rejected by Parliament; the movement split between moral-force and physical-force wings; economic improvement after 1848 reduced support.
items.misconception: Chartism failed because no one supported it.
items.random_wrong: Chartism failed because Queen Victoria personally banned the movement.
items.why: Chartism had millions of signatures and was hugely supported. It failed politically, not for lack of support — and five of its six points were eventually adopted.

items.q: Which 1933 law allowed Hitler to legislate without the Reichstag?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Enabling Act. “Reichstag Fire Decree” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.correct: German invasion of the USSR in June 1941
items.why: It's German invasion of the USSR in June 1941. “German invasion of Poland in 1939” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Approximately how many Jews were murdered in the Holocaust (in millions)?
items.why: The real figure is 6. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
